title: Jesus' Galilean Ministry
description: A curated teaching route through representative locations from the Galilean ministry narratives.
caution: The ministry itinerary is reconstructed from multiple Gospel accounts and should be used as a teaching outline rather than a single day-by-day travel log.

stops.name: Nazareth
stops.description: Jesus' hometown setting and early rejection scene.
stops.notes: A secure and familiar starting point.

stops.name: Capernaum
stops.description: A central base for Jesus' early ministry.
stops.notes: A core ministry hub in the Gospel narratives.

stops.name: Sea of Galilee
stops.description: The setting for teaching, miracles, and crossings.
stops.notes: A geographic center for the Galilean narratives.

stops.name: Nain
stops.description: The village of the widow's son.

stops.name: Gadara / Gerasa region
stops.description: A Gentile-region ministry setting on the eastern side of the lake.
stops.notes: The exact site depends on harmonization and manuscript tradition.

segments.label: Early ministry base
segments.description: Jesus moves from Nazareth toward Capernaum.
segments.caution: The move is well attested but not exhaustively timed.

segments.label: By the lake
segments.description: Teaching and miracles around the sea.
segments.caution: A general region is represented by a single line.

segments.label: Healing work
segments.description: The ministry extends to nearby villages.
segments.caution: The Gospels do not provide a continuous travel diary.

segments.label: Crossing the lake
segments.description: A movement to the eastern shore region.

segments.label: Northward teaching
segments.description: The narrative shifts to the northern borderlands.
segments.caution: The route is reconstructed for teaching value.
